title: Grande Bleu Jeju Yacht: Honest Sunset Cruise Review 2024
content:
Published: Today · Last updated: Today · By Grande Bleu Yacht Crew · Verified Local Operator

Grande Bleu is Korea's only certified catamaran in Jeju, offering 60-minute sunset cruises from Daepo Port with free unlimited food and drinks.

That's the short answer. If you're planning a Jeju honeymoon or a trip with friends this October and you're tired of generic "stunning" travel posts, here's an honest breakdown of what the Grande Bleu Jeju yacht actually delivers — and whether it's worth the price.

Quick Facts

  • Location: Daepo Port, Seogwipo, Jeju Island, South Korea (GPS 33.2287°N 126.4318°E)
  • Duration: 60 minutes
  • Capacity: 91 guests across two catamarans (44 + 47)
  • Price: ₩60,000 luxury / ₩70,000 sunset (adult) — about USD $45–52
  • Includes: Fishing gear, draft beer, wine, Jeju tangerine juice, ramyeon, snacks — all free, all unlimited
  • Departs from: Daepo Port, 5 minutes from Jungmun Resort Complex
  • Operating since: 2011 · 100,000+ guests served

Why Jeju Beats Bali for a Slow Honeymoon

Jeju Island sits off the southern coast of South Korea — volcanic, UNESCO-listed, and roughly the size of Maui. Most first-time visitors stick to the east-coast cafés or the west-coast beaches, then end up at Jungmun and call it a day.

That's a mistake. The real Seogwipo coastline starts where the tour buses stop. It's the slow side of K-culture: no crowds, no queues, just basalt cliffs dropping straight into the sea.

If you've already done Bali villas and Maldives overwater bungalows, Jeju in autumn (September to November) feels different. Cooler air, golden light by 5pm, and a sailing scene that's somehow still under-the-radar. The Jeju yacht experience is the easiest way to see it from the angle nobody photographs from land.

FAQ

How much does the Jeju yacht cost? The Grande Bleu sunset cruise is ₩70,000 per adult (~USD $52) and ₩40,000 per child. The daytime luxury tour is ₩60,000 adult. Prices include all food, drinks, and fishing — no hidden fees.

How long is the Jeju sunset cruise? The cruise runs exactly 60 minutes from departure to return at Daepo Port. Arrive 30 minutes early to check in and board.

Is food included on the Jeju yacht? Yes — all food and drinks are free and unlimited. This includes Korean draft beer, red and white wine, Jeju tangerine juice, bottled water, ramyeon cooked onboard, and local snacks.

Where does the Jeju yacht depart from? Grande Bleu departs from Daepo Port in Seogwipo, southern Jeju Island (GPS 33.2287°N 126.4318°E). It's a 5-minute drive from Jungmun Resort Complex and about 50 minutes from Jeju International Airport.

Is the Jeju catamaran safe for kids and seniors? Yes. The twin-hull catamaran design is far more stable than monohull boats, so seasickness is rare. A licensed captain and engineer are onboard every sailing, and life jackets are provided for all guests.

What's the best time for a sunset cruise in Jeju? October is ideal — mild weather, sunset around 5:30pm, and clear visibility of Hallasan from the deck. Book the sunset slot (not the daytime luxury slot) to catch the 360-degree golden hour view.
